title: Second ambush in the Graveyard

A '''battle''' in [[the Graveyard]] was the result of an ambush mounted by [[Ysanne Isard]] against [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] during the [[Bacta War]].

==Background==
After the successful [[Battle of Qretu 5|strike]] at [[Qretu 5]], Rogue Squadron launched a hit-and-fade campaign against the Thyferrans [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] shipments. In each attack, the Rogues showed up only for the time needed to force the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] to scramble their fighters, launch some [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] to take out a few [[TIE series/Legends|TIEs]] and then scatter. As this tactic was costing the [[Bacta Cartel/Legends|Bacta Cartel]] in both credits and prestige, [[Ysanne Isard]] attempted to mount an ambush against the Rogues. Thanks to [[Fliry Vorru]], Isard was able to locate the rendezvous point in the [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] system where the Rogues were to receive a big shipment of munitions and spare parts from [[Talon Karrde]]'s group. An [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruiser]] leased by Isard, from [[High Admiral]] [[Treuten Teradoc]], was intended to ensure that the Rogues would not be able to escape from the battle zone.

==The battle==
[[The Graveyard]] was the place chosen for the rendezvous between the twelve freighters led by ''[[Pulsar Skate]]'' and escorted by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] and its allies and Karrde's half-dozen ships led by [[Quelev Tapper]] on the ''[[Starry Ice]]''. As the two groups were beginning the transfer operation, the Interdictor cruiser ''[[Aggregator]]'' and the [[Victory II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Corrupter/Legends|''Corrupter'']] left their concealed position. The ''Corrupter'' positioned itself between the Interdictor and the Rebel [[Snubfighter/Legends|snubfighters]], begun to launch its [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and opened fire against the freighters. [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] led the Rogues in the first run of against the ''Corrupter'', while [[Tal'dira]]'s squadron covered them from the TIE starfighters. The proton torpedoes volley collapsed the ship's port shield and some of the torpedoes hit the destroyer's hull, blasting apart armor plates and crushing turbolaser batteries. To protect its unshielded flank, the ship rotated on itself to present the starboard shields to the attackers.

While [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] was nearing to order the launch the second volley, the ''Valiant'', a [[Thranta-class War Cruiser|''Thranta''-class War Cruiser]], ranged up from the Graveyard and opened fire against the unshielded flank of the ''Corrupter''. The second proton torpedoes volley then arrived adding, up its damage to the doomed ship. Wedge Antilles waited to launch his second pair of proton torpedoes until he was able to target the bridge of the ship, and upon impact his torpedos destroyed it. With its bridge gone, the hull of the ''Corrupter'' was left drifting down into the Graveyard. The ''Valiant'' then attacked the ''Aggregator''{{'s}} starboard shield, while the Rogues launched proton torpedoes against its port shield. With its shield beginning to collpase in several points, the ''Aggregator'' jumped away without waiting to pick up the surviving TIE fighters and the escape pods launched from the ''Corrupter''.

==Aftermath==
After the battle, Tycho Celchu reported to Commander Antilles that the mysterious ship was the [[Valiant (Thranta-class War Cruiser)|''Valiant'']], an automated [[Thranta-class War Cruiser|''Thranta''-class War Cruiser]] originally slaved to the [[Alderaanian War Frigate]] ''[[Another Chance]]'' as one of its escort ships. Having lost contact with ''Another Chance'', the ''Valiant'' had returned to the Graveyard and was waiting for the return of its flagship. As the [[Transponder code/Legends|IFF]] of Captain Celchu was transmitting the ''Another Chance's'' code and his fighter was broadcasting tactical targeting data, the ''Valiant'' had picked them and joined the fight.

Antilles also had [[Booster Terrik]] inquire about black market interest in a "slightly used" Interdictor cruiser, so as to give High Admiral Teradoc the implication that Rogue Squadron would attempt to capture his cruisers if he leased them to Isard again. Thus, the High Admiral formally withdrew [[Greater Maldrood|his faction's]] support to Ysanne Isard; this discouraged other Warlords from supporting Isard.
